## What is a [[Notes/Calculus III/Vectors|Vectors]] Function?

The following equation represents the form of a standard vector function

$$
\mathbf{r}(t) = \langle x(t), y(t), z(t) \rangle
$$

Where inputs are real numbers, and outputs are vectors.

(similar to parametric equations from calculus 2)

Sometimes, t is restricted to some parameter domain, eg:

$$
0 \leq t \leq 2\pi
$$

$$
t \in [0, 2\pi]
$$

**Lets graph a vector function**
Where 
	x = cos t a
	y = sin t
	between 0 and infinity
	

![[Screenshot 2026-01-21 at 4.10.26 PM.png|400]]

This equation yields a "helix" shape, one of the primary shapes that come from a vector function

**Example 2:**
Graph this equation:

$$
 \mathbf{r}(t) = \langle x_0, y_0, z_0 \rangle + t \langle a, b, c \rangle 
$$

$$
line = \langle x_0 + at, y_0 + bt, z_0 + ct \rangle
$$

For the line segment p to q, using example NumPy arrays:

```Python
p = np.array([1, 2, 3])
q = np.array([4, 5, 6])
```

Resultant Graph

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Curve of Intersection of Two Surfaces

Find a vector function `(parameterization)` of the curve of intersection of the surfaces:

$$
x^2 + y^2 = 1
$$

$$
y + z = 2
$$

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Interactive 3d Graph of the Visualization

<iframe src="https://www.desmos.com/3d/9zvk6m9ss7" width=660 height=400></iframe>

### Another Example

Visualization of the following equations

$$
4y = x^2 + z^2
$$

$$
y = x
$$

<iframe src="https://www.desmos.com/3d/vneotebpsa" width=660 height=400></iframe>
